transmittal.name: Transmittal Notification
transmittal.description: Sent when a transmittal is issued to recipients.
transmittal.subject: Transmittal Notification for {{documentNumberToken}}
transmittal.body:
Dear {{displayNameToken}},

Here is the list of documents in this transmittal:

{{startDocumentListToken}}
{{documentTitleToken}} - {{revisionToken}}
{{endDocumentListToken}}

Regards,
Doc Control

reviewAssigned.name: Review Assigned
reviewAssigned.description: Sent when a review task is assigned to a user.
reviewAssigned.subject: Review Assigned: {{activityNameToken}}
reviewAssigned.body:
Hello {{displayNameToken}},

You have been assigned {{activityNameToken}} in {{workspaceNameToken}}.
Due: {{dueDateToken}}

Open item: {{entryLinkToken}}

editor.detailsDescription: Subject supports tokens. Body supports rich text and token chips. Tokens are replaced at send time.
editor.footerGuidance: Tokens are dynamically replaced when notifications are generated. Rich text is rendered per channel, and template structure is designed for future localization, branding, and audit history.

packages.title: Title
packages.packagesConcept: A Package Object is a flexible collection of documents pulled from anywhere in the project - folders, search results, saved views, or a document register. Folders organise source documents; Packages organise deliverables.
packages.createSubtitle: Build a flexible package that draws documents from anywhere in the project.
packages.addDocumentsSubtitle: Add documents from any source. They don't need to live in the same folder.
packages.noDocumentsYet: No documents yet. Pick from anywhere in the project.
packages.excludedHelp: Excluded documents stay attached as static links to the source document but are not rendered into the package PDF.
packages.generateHelp: On generate, the coversheet is created immediately. Document rendering runs asynchronously and the package becomes available in the Packages library. The package is not stored in any folder - it lives in the Packages library.
packages.folderSourceHelp: Browse the folder tree and pick documents from anywhere.
packages.searchSourceHelp: Results for: "pump skid" - across all folders.
packages.registerSourceHelp: Document register filtered by discipline and area.
packages.pasteNumbers: Paste document numbers, one per line.
packages.noDocumentsInPackage: No documents in this package yet.
packages.noChangesDetected: No changes detected. The package is up to date with its source documents.
packages.sendViaTransmittalDesc: Issue this package as a controlled transmittal to internal or external recipients.
packages.startWorkflowDesc: Route this package through a multi-step review and approval workflow.
packages.downloadAsPdfDesc: Single PDF with coversheet, sections and rendered documents in package order.
packages.downloadAsZipDesc: ZIP archive containing the coversheet PDF and all included documents.
packages.sharePackageLinkDesc: Generate a controlled link for stakeholders to view this package.
packages.overviewStatusUpToDateDesc: The package matches the latest source documents.
packages.overviewStatusOutOfDateDesc: Source documents have changed since this package was generated. Repackage to create a new version.

chat.relatedDocumentsIntro: Here are some relevant documents I found related to your query:

suggestions.specs: What specifications govern this document set?
suggestions.changes: What changed in the latest revision?
suggestions.compliance: Are there any compliance risks here?

conversations.tagPv101Q1: What documents are associated with TAG PV-101?
conversations.tagPv101A1Intro: PV-101 (Knock-out Drum) is referenced in 6 controlled documents:
conversations.tagPv101A1Doc1: P-1001-PID-001 - Cooling Water Loop P&ID (Rev C)
conversations.tagPv101A1Footer: All references are in folders /Process/PIDs and /Mechanical/Area 01/GA.
conversations.tagPv101A2: No, all current revisions are the latest. SPEC-EQUIP-017 has a draft Rev 2 in progress with Kevin White but not yet released.
conversations.latestPidQ1: What's the latest revision of P-1001-PID-001?
conversations.latestPidA1: The latest released revision is Rev C (Approved on 28 Apr 2026 by Sarah Chen). Rev D is currently in review with the lead process engineer.
conversations.latestPidQ2: Show me the change between B and C.
conversations.latestPidA2: Between Rev B to Rev C: cooling water return line re-routed to header HDR-04, valve XV-3402 added downstream of E-201, line size on 4"-CW-02 increased to 6". Redline PDF available in version history.
conversations.pumpsQ1: List the pumps on drawing M-3012-GA-006.
conversations.pumpsA1Intro: Drawing M-3012-GA-006 (Pump Skid 06 GA) shows 4 pumps:
conversations.vendorPumpTitle: Vendor datasheets for centrifugal pump P-204A
conversations.pendingReviewTitle: Documents pending review on Substation A
conversations.ifcTitle: All IFC documents for Mechanical Area 01
conversations.whereUsedTitle: Where is valve tag XV-3402 referenced?
conversations.redlinesTitle: Open redlines on Civil Foundations package
conversations.transmittalsTitle: Transmittals issued to Contractor X this month

feedback.question: How's
feedback.thankYouSub: It helps us improve the experience.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: Our color system is built on a foundation of accessibility and flexibility. We use abstract naming conventions (Primary, Secondary, Neutral) to ensure the system remains resilient to future rebranding.

primary.title: Primary Palette
primary.subtitle: Cobalt Blue - Brand Identity & Action
primary.do1: Use Primary 500 for main navigation backgrounds
primary.do2: Use Primary 500 for primary call-to-action buttons
primary.do4: Use Primary 50-100 for subtle backgrounds in active states
primary.dont1: Don't use for destructive actions (use Error)
primary.dont2: Don't use for body text (use Neutral)

secondary.title: Secondary Palette
secondary.subtitle: Sky Blue - Decoration & Accents
secondary.constraintBody: Never use Secondary colors for text content. These shades are reserved for decorative elements, icons, and background states only.

whites.title: Whites
whites.body: Use Ice White for containers to subtly differentiate from the main White background.

semantic.title: Semantic Palette
semantic.subtitle: Feedback & States
semantic.errorBody: Used for critical errors, destructive actions, and validation failures.
semantic.warningBody: Used for non-critical warnings, attention needed, and pending states.
semantic.successBody: Used for completion, valid states, and positive feedback.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Matrix
accessibility.subtitle: WCAG 2.2 AA Compliance Check
accessibility.noteBody: We aim for AA Large (3.0:1) for graphics/large text and AA (4.5:1) for normal text.
